content.full_text:
May 26, 1977

William J. Taylor Jr.
Assistant Superintendent-
Business Affairs
Delano Union School District
P.O. Box 1117
Delano, Calif. 93215

Dear Mr. Taylor:

Your letter of April 29, 1977, to the Chairman of the Fair Political Practices Commission, has been referred to me.

You requested information regarding the procedure for exemptions from disclosure and campaign reporting.

The Political Reform Act requires all public officials and candidates for public office to file disclosure statements (see Gov. Code §§87200-208). There is no provision which allows a person who is designated under Chapter 7 of the Act to fail to file a disclosure statement.

The Fair Political Practices Commission last year promulgated a regulation which provides a procedure for certain candidates and campaign committees to request an exemption from some of the campaign reporting provisions of Chapter 4 of the Act. This procedure was initiated at the direction of the United States Supreme Court outlined in Buckley v. Valeo. Enclosed please find a copy of the Commission's regulation, 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18429, which outline this exemption procedure.

Also enclosed for your information is a copy of the Political Reform Act of 1974 as Amended.

If you have any questions or require any further information, please feel free to contact me.

Sincerely,

Jeanne Pritchard

April 29, 1977

California Fair Political Practices Commission
Mr. Lowenstein, Chairman
Sacramento, California

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

I would like to obtain information on the grounds upon which an official may fail, without penalty, to file a Disclosure Statement under the Conflict of Interest Referendum Amendment, or a statement of campaign expenditures and contributions.

I heard part of your interview on a TV station while in Modesto on April 23, 1977, related to this.

If you can supply such information, or direct me to a source, I will appreciate it.

Sincerely Yours,

[Signature]
WILLIAM J. TAYLOR, JR.
Assistant Superintendent-Business Affairs
